content.full_text:
Fern Por-rrrcel PnacrrcEs Couurs sroN
428 J Street. Suite 620. Sacramento, CA g5gl4-2329
(916) 322-5660. p21 (916) 322-0886
October 78,2002
Colin J. Coffey, General Counsel
Archer. Norris
Post Office Box 8035
Walnut Creek, CA 94596-3728
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-02-264
Dear Mr. Coffey:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Donald Newman,
M.D. regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').1 The Act requires
the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are
in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 8311a@); regulation
18329(a) (copy enclosed).) However, the Commission may decline to provide written
advice where the requestor is seeking advice that requires us to evaluate conduct that has
alteady occurred. (Regulation 18329(b)(8XA).) In addition, the Commission may also
decline to provide written advice if it would be "inappropriate or otherwise not in the
public interest" to do so. (Regulation 18329(bX8XF).)
Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by
the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Siction
83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we
decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are
currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In
addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions,
some of which have akeady occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to
past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of
the Act so mandated by section 831 1 1.
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice
from you or private counsel.
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
L::


Should you have any further questions, please contact the commission at
File No. W-02-264
Page No. 2
(et6)
322-5660.
Enclosure
cwje

Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
West
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion: The Commission declines to provide written advice at this time because the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, and the request involves analysis of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, making it inappropriate to issue advice that would relate to past conduct.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought written advice on behalf of Donald Newman, M.D. regarding compliance with the Political Reform Act, but the specific questions are not detailed in the document.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because similar issues are under pending investigation and the request involves past conduct, which would not further the impartial administration of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter with clear reasoning provided. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers, but substantive explanation of declination grounds is present. Requestor identity and subject matter are identifiable.

QUESTION: The requestor sought written advice on behalf of Donald Newman, M.D. regarding compliance with the Political Reform Act, but the specific questions are not detailed in the document.

CONCLUSION: The Commission declines to provide written advice at this time because the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, and the request involves analysis of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, making it inappropriate to issue advice that would relate to past conduct.

embedding.summary: This is a declination letter from the FPPC declining to provide written advice to counsel for Donald Newman, M.D. regarding Political Reform Act compliance, citing pending investigation and analysis of past conduct as reasons for the declination.
